questions.question: Question count: 14 Note: Best-effort structured extraction from a column-formatted source PDF. Where the source itself marked an answer as changed, that note is preserved.

Q1. (2021) With reference to ‘palm oil’, consider the following statements: [2021] 1) The palm oil tree is native to Southeast Asia. 2) The palm oil is a raw material for some industries producing lipstick and perfumes. 3) The palm oil can be used to produce biodiesel. Which of the statements given above are correct? Source ref: gk_part1 / Q26
Q2. (2021) The term ‘ACE2’ is talked about in the context of: [2021] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q27

options.A: Genes introduced in the genetically modified plants
options.B: Development of India’s own satellite navigation system

questions.question: Q3. (2021) ‘Triclosan’, considered harmful when exposed to high levels for a long time, is most likely present in which of the following? [2021] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q28
Q4. (2016) Recently, for the first time in our country, which of the following States has declared a particular butterfly as ‘State Butterfly’? [2016] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q47
Q5. (2011) Recently, “oil zapper’’ was in the news. What is it? [2011] Source ref: gk_part1 / Q63

options.A: It is an eco-friendly technology for the remediation of oily sludge and oil spills.
options.B: It is the latest technology developed for under-sea oil exploration.
options.C: It is a genetically engineered high biofuel-yielding maize variety.
options.D: It is the latest technology to control the accidentally caused flames from oil wells.

questions.question: Q6. (2010) With reference to the treatment of cancerous tumors, a tool called cyber knife has been making the news. In this context, which one of the following statements is not correct? [2010] Source ref: gk_part2 / Q1

options.A: It is a robotic image guided system.
options.B: It delivers an extremely precise dose of radiation.
options.C: It has the capability of achieving sub - millimeter accuracy.
options.D: It can map the spread of tumour in the body.

questions.question: Q7. (2010) Recently, LASIK (Laser Assisted In Situ Keratomileusis) procedure is being made popular for vision correction. Which one of the following statements in this context is not correct? [2010] Source ref: gk_part2 / Q2

options.A: LASIK procedure is used to correct refractive errors of the eye.
options.B: It is a procedure that permanently changes the shapes of the cornea.
options.C: It reduces a person’s dependence on glasses or contact lenses.
options.D: It is a procedure that can be done on the person of any age.

questions.question: Q8. (2010) Mon 863 is a variety of maize. It was in the news for the following reason: [2010] Source ref: gk_part2 / Q3

options.A: It is a genetically modified dwarf variety which is resistant to drought.
options.B: It is a genetically modified variety which is past resistant.
options.C: It is a genetically modified variety with ten times higher protein content than regular maize crop.
options.D: It is a genetically modified variety used exclusively for bio - fuel production.

questions.question: Q9. (2009) Consider the following: [2009] 1. Camphor 2. Chicory 3. Vanilla Which of the above is/are plant product (s)? Source ref: gk_part2 / Q41
Q10. (2008) The release of which one of the following into ponds and wells helps in controlling the mosquitoes? [2008] Source ref: gk_part2 / Q43
Q11. (2006) Which one of the following pairs is not correctly matched? [2006] Source ref: gk_part3 / Q20
Q12. (2005) Which one of the following is the landmark performance of Dr. V. Mohan Reddy, an Indian born Doctor working in USA, during February, 2003? [2005] Source ref: gk_part3 / Q71

options.A: Research on human stem cell which is likely to revolutionise treatment of deadly disease like cancer.
options.B: Successful open heart procedure called arterial switch performed on an infant.
options.C: Research on genetic engineering which can help in treatment of deadly disease like AIDS.
options.D: Successful brain surgery to revive the function of nearly dead brain of a premature born infant.

questions.question: Q13. (2004) Tarun Bharat Sangh, an organization based in a village near Alwar (Rajasthan), has become famous because of: [2004] Source ref: gk_part3 / Q91

options.B: Rehabilitation of women victims of AIDS
options.C: Livelihood projects for destitute rural women

questions.question: Q14. (1995) Coffee cultivation in Sri Lanka was abandoned because of the disease: [1995] Source ref: gk_part4 / Q54
